items.text: Describe semantic and numerical harmonization steps and unresolved issues, if using multi-source data and data types (e.g., raw data files, spreadsheets, published literature).
Provide processing code that regenerates the ML-ready dataset from source data, and clearly state how dataset equivalence is checked (record counts/IDs, etc).
Define all input features/descriptors and their generation pipeline.
Provide dataset identifiers, access details, and core metadata sufficient for reuse.
State policy on negative/failed/suboptimal experiments and include if informative.
Document full model specification (architecture, hyperparameters, objective, versions).
Describe data-split strategies implemented to prevent information leakage.
Clearly state metrics applied to assess model performance with confidence intervals to avoid best-only reporting.
Justify the choice of metrics and examine trade-offs explicitly for multi-objective tasks.
Describe the uncertainty quantification method, its calibration and utilization.

Specify the agent policy and objectives, constraints, acquisition or reward functions, and provide configuration details or pseudocode with versioning including sources of stochasticity and which parameters are fixed across runs.
Declare the level of autonomy and human oversight, and document any interventions or overrides during the campaign, including their frequency, rationale, and impact on subsequent agent behavior.
Define stopping criteria and anomaly or failure handling procedures, and report any aborted runs, exclusions, or post hoc corrections.
Describe safety bounds, interlocks, and shutdown mechanisms, and explain how the agent is prevented from executing unsafe experiments.

Maintain a traceable record of the campaign trajectory, with summaries or representative segments when full iteration-level detail is impractical, sufficient to support the statistical reproducibility of agent behavior.

items.section: Tutorials, Notebooks, and Other Executable Artifacts
items.text: Provide executable tutorials or notebooks illustrating key elements of data management, model development, and workflow integration.

items.section: Tutorials, Notebooks, and Other Executable Artifacts
items.text: Link each tutorial or notebook to the relevant manuscript sections and checklist items, and include versioning information where feasible.

items.section: Tutorials, Notebooks, and Other Executable Artifacts
items.text: Document the execution environment required to run the tutorials (e.g., container images, lockfiles, or environment specifications) to mitigate dependency and API drift.
